Spigelia Anthelmintica.personality: A debilitated, run-down individual who is highly sensitive to cold, atmospheric changes, and all forms of stimulation including noise, light, motion, and even mental exertion. They sit transfixed and still, avoiding movement because any jarring or activity intensifies their suffering.
Spigelia Anthelmintica.mental_state: Weak memory with marked disinclination to work and an inability to concentrate, as even thinking aggravates their complaints. Restless and anxious with solicitude about the future, yet paradoxically may sit motionless to avoid provoking pain. So nervous at times that they feel they must fly, full of excitement, unable to keep still or maintain self-control.
Spigelia Anthelmintica.emotional_pattern: Gloomy with a suicidal mood, easily irritated or offended by the slightest provocation. Harbors peculiar fears, especially of pointed things such as pins, and carries a deep anxiety and apprehension about the future. The intensity of their suffering leaves its mark both physically and emotionally, producing prostration, cold sweat, and a state of nervous exhaustion.
